request.question: Why was O.J Mayo, named the Most Valuable Player at the 2007 Anaheim Classic dismissed from the NBA?

title: Scott Niedermayer
Jerry Lucas
Simona Gioli
Russell Westbrook
2007 Anaheim Classic
2013 NBA Summer League
Leanne Del Toso
Kevin Durant
CBA Most Valuable Player
O. J. Mayo
sentences: Scott Niedermayer (born August 31, 1973) is a Canadian former ice hockey defenceman and current Special Assignment Coach of the Anaheim Ducks.
He played 18 seasons and over 1,000 games in the National Hockey League (NHL) for the New Jersey Devils and Anaheim Ducks.
Niedermayer is a four-time Stanley Cup champion and played in five NHL All-Star Games.
He won the James Norris Memorial Trophy in 2003–04 as the NHL's top defenceman and the Conn Smythe Trophy in 2007 as the most valuable player of the playoffs.
On January 27, 2017, in a ceremony during the All-Star Weekend in Los Angeles, Niedermayer was part of the second group of players to be named one of the '100 Greatest NHL Players' in history.
Jerry Ray Lucas (born March 30, 1940) is an American former basketball player and memory education expert.

He was a nationally-awarded high school player, national college star at Ohio State, and 1960 gold medal Olympian and international player before starring as a professional player in the National Basketball Association (NBA).
As a collegian, Lucas led the Ohio State Buckeyes to the 1960 college national championship and three straight NCAA finals.
He remains today the only three-time Big Ten Player of the Year, and was also twice named NCAA Player of the Year.

As a professional, Lucas was named All-NBA First Team three times, a NBA All-Star seven times, was 1964 NBA Rookie of the Year, and was named Most Valuable Player of the 1965 NBA All-Star Game among other honors and awards.
He was inducted to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 1980.
Simona Gioli (born September 17, 1977 in Rapallo) is a volleyball player from Italy.
Currently, she plays for Galatasaray in Turkey.
Gioli was a member of the Women's National Team that won the gold medal at the 2007 European Championship in Belgium and Luxembourg.
Gioli was named "Most Valuable Player and ""Best Blocker" at the 2007 FIVB Women's World Cup and 2009 FIVB Women's World Grand Champions Cup.
She was also awarded "MVP" at the 2006-07 CEV Cup won by her team Sirio Perugia.
She won the 2007–08 CEV Indesit Champions League with Sirio Perugia and also was individually awarded "Most Valuable Player".
Russell Westbrook III (born November 12, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Oklahoma City Thunder of the National Basketball Association (NBA).
He is a six-time NBA All-Star, and a two-time NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player, winning consecutive awards in 2015 and 2016.
He is also a six-time All-NBA Team member and led the league in scoring in 2014–15 and 2016–17.
In 2017, Westbrook became one of two players in NBA history to average a triple-double for a season, along with Oscar Robertson in 1962.
He also set a record for the most triple-doubles in a season, with 42.
He was subsequently named the 2016–17 NBA Most Valuable Player.
The 2007 Anaheim Classic was played between November 22 and November 25, 2007 at the Anaheim Convention Center in Anaheim, California.
The champion of the tournament was Southern California, who defeated Southern Illinois in the Championship Game.
The Most Valuable Player was O. J. Mayo of Southern California.
The 2013 NBA Summer League is a pro basketball league run by the NBA just after the 2013 NBA draft.
It gives newly drafted players a chance to test their skills against each other, and to give them a feel for professional basketball.
All 30 NBA teams participated, along with the D-League Select.
The Miami Heat were the only team to participate in both Summer Leagues.
It ran from July 7–12 in Orlando and July 12–22 in Las Vegas.
Jeremy Lamb of the Oklahoma City Thunder was named the Most Valuable Player of the Orlando Summer League.
Jonas Valančiūnas of the Toronto Raptors went on to be named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League.
Ian Clark of the Golden State Warriors was named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League Championship Game.
Leanne Del Toso (born 12 August 1980) is a 3.5 point wheelchair basketball player who represented Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London, where she won a silver medal.
Diagnosed with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy at the age of nineteen, Del Toso started playing wheelchair basketball in 2006.
Playing in the local Victorian competition, she was named the league's most valuable player in 2007.
That year started playing for the Knox Ford Raiders in the Women's National Wheelchair Basketball League (WNWBL).
The following year, she was named the team's Players' Player and Most Valuable Player (MVP).
Kevin Wayne Durant (born September 29, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Golden State Warriors of the National Basketball Association (NBA).

He has won an NBA championship, an NBA Most Valuable Player Award, the Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award, the NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player Award, four NBA scoring titles, the NBA Rookie of the Year Award, and two Olympic gold medals.
Durant has also been selected to seven All-NBA teams and eight NBA All-Star teams.
The Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) MVP is the annual award that is handed out at the end of each Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) regular season to the league's most valuable player.
From the league's inaugural 1995–96 season until the 2011–12 season, only Chinese/Taiwanese players were eligible to win the award.
Since the 2012–13 season, separate awards for both domestic and international players are handed out.
Domestic players are eligible to win the CBA Domestic Most Valuable Player award, while international players are eligible to win the CBA International Most Valuable Player award.
Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (born November 5, 1987) is an American professional basketball player.
He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.
The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits.
Mayo entered the 2008 NBA draft and was selected by the Minnesota Timberwolves with the third overall pick.
He was later traded to the Memphis Grizzlies, with whom he played four seasons.
In 2011, he tested positive for a banned steroid and was suspended by the NBA for 10 games.
Mayo signed with the Dallas Mavericks in 2012, and then with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2013.
On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

It gives newly drafted players a chance to test their skills against each other, and to give them a feel for professional basketball.  All 30 NBA teams participated, along with the D-League Select. Jonas Valančiūnas of the Toronto Raptors went on to be named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League. He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.  The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits. On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

response.summary_1:
- Fact: [1] quoted: "The Most Valuable Player was O. J. Mayo of Southern California."
- Fact: [6] quoted: "On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program."
- Fact: [6] paraphrase: In 2011 Mayo "tested positive for a banned steroid" and received a 10-game NBA suspension.
- Bridge entity: O. J. Mayo = Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (passage [6]) — unambiguous match to the question.
- Missing/ambiguous facts: None needed to answer the question (dismissal reason is stated).
- Final answer (supported): He was dismissed for violating the NBA's anti-drug program (passage [6]).
response.hop2_query: O. J. Mayo July 1 2016 dismissal reason NBA anti-drug program
hop2_docs: O. J. Mayo | Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (born November 5, 1987) is an American professional basketball player.  He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.  The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits.  Mayo entered the 2008 NBA draft and was selected by the Minnesota Timberwolves with the third overall pick.  He was later traded to the Memphis Grizzlies, with whom he played four seasons.  In 2011, he tested positive for a banned steroid and was suspended by the NBA for 10 games.  Mayo signed with the Dallas Mavericks in 2012, and then with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2013.  On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

Mars Rafikov | Mars Zakirovich Rafikov (Russian: Марс Закирович Рафиков , 29 September 1933 – 23 July 2000) was a Soviet cosmonaut who was dismissed from the Soviet space program for disciplinary reasons.

High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area | The High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area program (HIDTA) is a drug-prohibition enforcement program run by the United States Office of National Drug Control Policy.  It was established in 1990 after the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988 was passed.

Sérgio Henrique Ferreira | Sérgio Henrique Ferreira (October 4, 1934 – July 17, 2016) was a Brazilian physician and pharmacologist noted for the discovery of the bradykinin potentiating factor, which led to new and widely used anti-hypertension drugs — the ACE inhibitors.

Unfair dismissal in the United Kingdom | Unfair dismissal in the United Kingdom is the part of UK labour law that requires fair, just and reasonable treatment by employers in cases where a person's job could be terminated.  The Employment Rights Act 1996 regulates this by saying that employees are entitled to a fair reason before being dismissed, based on their capability to do the job, their conduct, whether their position is economically redundant, on grounds of a statute, or some other substantial reason.  It is automatically unfair for an employer to dismiss an employee, regardless of length of service, for becoming pregnant, or for having previously asserted certain specified employment rights.  Otherwise, an employee must have worked for two years.  This means an employer only terminates an employee's job lawfully if the employer follows a fair procedure, acts reasonably and has a fair reason.  Contrary to the gov.uk website, it is not automatically unfair for an employer to dismiss an employee for a discriminatory reason protected by the Equality Act 2010 (with the exception of pregnancy); there is no statutory provision for this and it just amounts to ordinary discrimination.

Ivan Anikeyev | Ivan Nikolayevich Anikeyev (Russian: Иван Николаевич Аникеев ; February 12, 1933 - August 8, 1992) was a Soviet cosmonaut who was dismissed from the Soviet space program for disciplinary reasons.

Iwate Big Bulls | The Iwate Big Bulls are a professional basketball team that compete in the second division of the Japanese B.League.  Due to financial reasons, all cheerleaders were dismissed in 2016.
hop2_titles: Unfair dismissal in the United Kingdom
response.summary_2: On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program. Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo tested positive for a banned steroid in 2011 and received a 10-game NBA suspension.
response.answer: He was dismissed for violating the NBA's anti-drug program; he had tested positive for a banned steroid in 2011 and received a 10-game suspension.
